title: Fusion (Philosophy)
wikitext:
'''Fusion''' — as a [[Clan Tho'ra|Tho'ra]] concept — extends far beyond [[Nuclear Fusion]] as a power source. It is the Tho'ra ''way'' of accessing [[Universal Magic]]: an artificial approach that constructs technology to interface with the fundamental forces of reality.

== The Word "Fusion" ==
In the world of [[Fusion Girl]], "Fusion" carries multiple simultaneous meanings:

* '''Nuclear Fusion''' — the literal power source at the core of every [[Fusion Engine Suit]], [[Fusion Drive]], and [[Fusion Cell]]. Physically and scientifically clear, easy to understand. Then the lore hits, and Fusion means a ''whole lot more.''
* '''Psionic + Technological''' — merging consciousness interfaces ([[Psi Tech]]) with hard engineering ([[Fusion Tech]])
* '''Organic + Artificial''' — biological neural networks linked to machine systems
* '''Universal Language + Physical Engineering''' — coding reality through constructed devices
* '''Natural + Constructed''' — building what the universe provides organically to others

== Fusion vs. Natura Magic ==
'''Fusion''' is the Tho'ra approach to power, technology, and reality — the way ''[[Natura Magic]]'' is [[Tarr'i]]'s approach.

{| class="wikitable"
! Aspect !! Fusion (Tho'ra Way) !! [[Natura Magic]] (Tarr'i's Way)
|-
| '''Access Method''' || Build technology to interface with [[Universal Language]] || Channel [[Universal Magic]] organically
|-
| '''Philosophy''' || Construct the [[Morphing Grid]] || Exist within it naturally
|-
| '''Metaphor''' || Engineering a radio to hear the signal || Being born able to hear it
|-
| '''Strength''' || Reproducible, scalable, shareable || Intuitive, holistic, personal
|-
| '''Limitation''' || Requires hardware, power, maintenance || Requires innate ability or training
|}

Both paths access the same underlying reality — [[Universal Language]] — but through fundamentally different means. Neither is superior; they are complementary.

== The Morphing Grid ==
{{Main|Morphing Grid}}
The Tho'ra approach to Fusion is essentially '''building a [[Morphing Grid]]''' — constructing the interface between technology and [[Universal Magic]]. Like Power Rangers constructing a Morpher to access the Morphing Grid's power, the Tho'ra build [[Fusion Engine Suit]]s, [[Fusion Wings]], [[Plasma Morphers]], and eventually [[Star Morphers]] to access universal forces through engineering.

[[Universal Language]] naturally exists as an emergent feature of the nature of the universe. [[Universal Magic]] is the raw form of the same thing. The Tho'ra ''make'' a doorway into it.

== See Also ==
* [[Fusion Tech]] — the technology family built on this philosophy
* [[Morphing Grid]] — the universal power framework
* [[Universal Language]] — the emergent phenomenon
* [[Universal Magic]] — the raw form
* [[Natura Magic]] — the organic alternative ([[Tarr'i]])
* [[Clan Tho'ra]] — the practitioners
* [[Fusion Engine Suit]] — the primary expression

[[Category:Concepts]]
[[Category:Fusion Tech]]
[[Category:Clan Tho'ra]]

title: Fusion Blades
wikitext:
{| class="infobox" style="width:300px; font-size:90%; border:1px solid #aaa; background:#f9f9f9; margin:0.5em 0 0.5em 1em; padding:0.2em; float:right;"
! colspan="2" style="background:#333; color:#fff;" | '''Fusion Blades'''
|-
| '''Type''' || Fusion-plasma energy melee weapon
|-
| '''Developer''' || [[Clan Tho'ra]]
|-
| '''Mechanism''' || Fusion reactions and plasma pockets create energy cutting edges
|-
| '''Mounting''' || Wrist-mounted (primary), handheld, suit-integrated
|-
| '''Power Source''' || [[Fusion Cells]] / [[Fusion Engine Suit]] power
|-
| '''Also Known As''' || Wrist Blades, Fusion Cutters (when used as tools)
|}

'''Fusion Blades''' (also called '''Wrist Blades''') are melee weapons that use fusion reactions and plasma pockets to create energy cutter blades. They are the signature close-combat weapons of the [[Fusion Engine Suit]].

== Mechanism ==
Fusion Blades generate a controlled plasma pocket shaped into a blade form. The plasma is sustained by a continuous micro-fusion reaction in the blade's emitter, creating a cutting edge of extreme temperature and energy density. The blade cuts through matter via thermal destruction — superheated plasma melts and vaporizes anything it contacts.

== Variants ==
{| class="wikitable"
! Variant !! Description !! Blade Type
|-
| '''Standard Fusion Blade''' || Wrist-mounted compact cutter || Hot plasma edge
|-
| '''[[Solid-Light Technology|Solid-Light]] Blade''' || Solid-light projected blade || Physical + energy hybrid
|-
| '''High-Frequency Bladed Enclosure''' || Physical blade containing a plasma core || Mechanical + plasma hybrid
|-
| '''Cold Plasma Blade''' || [[Cold Fusion]]-sustained low-temperature variant || Cold plasma edge (precision cutting)
|-
| '''Hyper Blade''' || [[Hyper Fusion]]-powered advanced variant || Dimensional-edge plasma
|}

== Fusion Blades vs. Related Weapons ==
* '''[[Psi Blades]]''' — use psionic fields to cut; no plasma involved
* '''[[Fusion Cutters]]''' — general-purpose tools that use the same technology as Fusion Blades but optimized for cutting materials rather than combat
* '''[[Super Blades]]''' — battle-tier designation for advanced blade weapons
* '''[[Fusion Wings]]''' — wing plasma pockets can be formed into sword shapes for cutting

== See Also ==
* [[Fusion Cutters]] — tool-grade version
* [[Psi Blades]] — psionic blade alternative
* [[Fusion Weapons]] — weapon technology family
* [[Plasma Morphers]] — can shape plasma into blade forms

title: Fusion Cells
wikitext:
'''Fusion Cell''' (also referred to as '''Fusion Power Cell''', '''Fusion Module''', or simply '''Cell''') is the most modular and portable component in Clan Tho'ra's advanced fusion-based power systems. It functions as a self-contained energy generation and storage unit that can be swapped in and out of vehicles, equipment, or base infrastructure.

{| class="infobox" style="width:300px; font-size:90%; border:1px solid #aaa; background:#f9f9f9; margin:0.5em 0 0.5em 1em; padding:0.2em; float:right;"
! colspan="2" | '''Fusion Cell'''
|-
| '''Type''' || Self-contained fusion energy module
|-
| '''Developer''' || [[Tho'ra Clan]] / [[Earth Intelligence Network]] (in-house R&D)
|-
| '''Manufacturer''' || In-house fabrication at [[Tho'ra HQ]]
|-
| '''Generation''' || Generation 1–3 (Flash Hydrogen to early Micro Fusion)
|-
| '''Introduction''' || 2032–2033 (Flash Hydrogen variants)  
2035–2038 (Micro Fusion variants)
|-
| '''Status''' || Operational (Flash) / Prototype (Micro Fusion)
|-
| '''Primary User''' || [[Hydro Speeder]] fleet, [[Magneto Speeder]] auxiliary systems, portable mission gear, Tho'ra HQ backup power
|-
| '''Role''' || Swappable power supply for low-to-medium demand systems
|-
| '''Power output''' || 600–5,000 W continuous (scalable via stacking)
|-
| '''Energy density''' || Flash: ~6 g H₂ / 100 g carrier  
Micro: Orders of magnitude higher (theoretical)
|-
| '''Dimensions''' || ~30 × 15 × 10 cm (standard cartridge size)
|-
| '''Weight''' || 8–25 kg (depending on type and capacity)
|}

== Overview ==
Fusion Cells are the building-block power modules of Clan Tho'ra technology. They can be inserted into vehicles, tools, or base systems to provide on-demand electricity or direct thrust. The earliest operational type is the [[Flash Hydrogen Fuel Cells]] (chemical hydrogen release), while later generations incorporate [[Micro Fusion Fuel Cells]] (true nuclear fusion).

Cells are designed for rapid field replacement, making them ideal for missions where downtime must be minimized and power needs vary.

== Design & Specifications ==
* '''Form factor''': Standardized cartridge or box shape for hot-swap compatibility
* '''Power output''': 600–5,000 W continuous (scalable by stacking multiple cells)
* '''Energy density''': Flash Hydrogen: ~6 g H₂ per 100 g carrier  
Micro Fusion: Extremely high (theoretical ~10^8–10^9 Wh/kg)
* '''Refuel / Recharge''': Flash: < 5 minutes (ambient water electrolysis)  
Micro: Years to decades (fusion fuel lasts the life of the cell)
* '''Dimensions''': ~30 × 15 × 10 cm (standard module)
* '''Weight''': 8–25 kg (Flash lighter, Micro heavier due to shielding)
* '''Exhaust''': Flash: Water vapor only  
Micro: Helium + low-level neutrons (early); near-zero radiation (target)
* '''Safety features''': Thermal runaway suppression, automatic disconnect, radiation shielding (Micro variants)

== Variants ==
* '''Flash Hydrogen Fusion Cell''' — Chemical hydrogen release into PEM fuel cell. Earliest and most widely deployed (2032–2035+).
* '''Micro Fusion Cell''' — True nuclear fusion. Experimental in 2035–2038, maturing by 2040+.
* '''Hybrid Cell''' — Flash + early Micro Fusion for transitional power (startup on Flash, sustain on Micro).

== Operational Use ==
* '''Hydro Speeder''' — Primary power source for surface propulsion and auxiliary systems.
* '''Magneto Speeder''' — Auxiliary / backup power for low-demand flight modes and electronics.
* '''Portable mission gear''' — HelmKits, symbology emitters, field sensors, and recovery packs.
* '''Tho'ra HQ backup power''' — Redundant power for life support, comms, and low-load fabrication during grid-down.
* '''Forward outposts''' — Easily transported and swapped power for temporary camps.

== Development History ==
* '''2032–2033''': Flash Hydrogen Fusion Cells enter operational use at Tho'ra HQ and Hydro Speeder prototypes.
* '''2035–2038''': First Micro Fusion Cells prototyped and tested in controlled environments.
* '''2038–2040''': Hybrid Flash + Micro cells deployed in Magneto Speeder auxiliary roles.
* '''2040+''': Micro Fusion Cells become the dominant high-energy module as alliance capabilities expand.

== Advantages & Limitations ==
* '''Advantages''':
** High modularity and hot-swap capability
** Rapid field replacement
** Scalable power via stacking
** Flash variants offer safe, fast refueling

* '''Limitations''':
** Flash: Lower total energy density than true fusion
** Micro: Still experimental, heavier, and radiation concerns in early variants
** Requires secure storage and handling (especially Micro)

== See also ==
* [[Fusion Drives]]
* [[Fusion Thrusters]]
* [[Fusion Turbines]]
* [[Fusion Engines]]
* [[Flash Hydrogen Fuel Cells]]
* [[Micro Fusion Fuel Cells]]
* [[Turbo Fusion]]
* [[Super Fusion]]
* [[Hyper Fusion]]
* [[Magneto Speeder]]
* [[Hydro Speeder]]
* [[Tho'ra HQ]]

title: Fusion Cutters
wikitext:
[[Fusion Tech]] - [[Fusion Cutters]]

{| class="infobox" style="width:300px; font-size:90%; border:1px solid #aaa; background:#f9f9f9; margin:0.5em 0 0.5em 1em; padding:0.2em; float:right;"
! colspan="2" style="background:#333; color:#fff;" | '''Fusion Cutters'''
|-
| '''Type''' || General-purpose fusion-edge cutting tools
|-
| '''Developer''' || [[Clan Tho'ra]]
|-
| '''Technology''' || [[Fusion Blades]] technology applied to tools
|-
| '''Applications''' || Engineering, salvage, construction, field repair, combat (improvised)
|-
| '''Related Weapons''' || [[Fusion Blades]], [[Psi Blades]]
|}

'''Fusion Cutters''' are general-purpose cutting tools that use the same fusion-plasma technology as [[Fusion Blades]], but optimized for material cutting, engineering, and field work rather than combat. They are named in the game's original concept as one of the core "Fusion" technologies that gives [[Fusion Girl]] her identity.

== Function ==
Fusion Cutters generate a controlled plasma edge for cutting through:
* Structural materials (metal, composite, stone)
* Salvage operations (dismantling equipment, vehicles)
* Field construction and repair
* Emergency breaching
* Improvised combat (they ''can'' be used as weapons, but are not optimized for it)

== Relationship to Weapons ==
Fusion Cutters are the '''tool-grade''' implementation of the same technology that [[Fusion Blades]] use for combat:

{| class="wikitable"
! Tool/Weapon !! Purpose !! Optimization
|-
| '''Fusion Cutters''' || Engineering / utility || Precision, safety, efficiency
|-
| '''[[Fusion Blades]]''' || Combat || Speed, damage, reach
|-
| '''[[Psi Blades]]''' || Combat (psionic) || Different tech base entirely
|}

== See Also ==
* [[Fusion Blades]] — combat version of the same tech
* [[Fusion Weapons]] — weapon family
* [[Plasma Morphers]] — plasma shaping system
* [[Fusion Tech]] — technology family

[[Category:Tho'ra Technology]]

title: Fusion Drive
wikitext:
{{Infobox
| title      = Fusion Drive
| image      =
| caption    = Integrated fusion propulsion assembly
| header1    = Overview
| label2     = Type
| data2      = Integrated fusion propulsion assembly
| label3     = Developer
| data3      = [[Clan Tho'ra]] / [[Earth Intelligence Network]]
| label4     = Generations
| data4      = Gen 1–3 (Flash H₂ → Micro Fusion → Aneutronic)
| label5     = Introduction
| data5      = [[2032]] (Hydro) · [[2035]] (Magneto)
| label6     = Status
| data6      = Operational through multiple variants
| header7    = Variants
| label8     = Flash Hydrogen
| data8      = NaBH₄ + PEM + water-jet (Hydro Speeder)
| label9     = Micro Fusion
| data9      = D-T / p-B11 + MHD thrust (Magneto Speeder)
| label10    = Turbo Fusion
| data10     = Exhaust-driven turbocompressor, sustained cruise
| label11    = Super Fusion
| data11     = Mechanical supercharger, instant power surge
| label12    = Hybrid Fusion
| data12     = Flash H₂ + Micro Fusion transitional
| header13   = Specifications
| label14    = Power Range
| data14     = 1.2 kW (Flash) → 500+ kW (Super Fusion)
| label15    = Thrust Range
| data15     = 2,000 N (Hydro water-jet) → 50 kN+ (MHD)
| label16    = Weight
| data16     = 150–400 kg (variant-dependent)
| label17    = Dimensions
| data17     = 1.5–2.5 m × 0.8–1.2 m × 0.8–1.2 m
| below      = ''Central propulsion for all [[Clan Tho'ra]] vehicles''
}}

The '''Fusion Drive''' is the complete, integrated propulsion subsystem used in [[Clan Tho'ra]] vehicles. It combines power generation ([[Fusion Cells]]), energy conversion, thrust generation ([[Fusion Thrusters]]), vectoring, and control electronics into a single major assembly.

The Fusion Drive is not a single design but a ''family'' of assemblies that evolve through the Tho'ra technology ladder:

== Variant Architecture ==

=== Flash Hydrogen Fusion Drive (Gen-1) ===
''Primary vehicle: [[Hydro Speeder]]''

Power chain:
<math>\text{NaBH}_4 \xrightarrow{\text{H}_2\text{O}} \text{H}_2 \xrightarrow{\text{PEM}} \text{e}^- \xrightarrow{\text{motor}} \text{impeller} \xrightarrow{\text{water-jet}} \text{thrust}</math>

* '''Power source''': [[Flash Hydrogen Fuel Cell]]s (600–1,200 W per module)
* '''Thrust mechanism''': Axial-flow water-jet impeller (Hamilton-type)
* '''Net thrust''': <math>T = \dot{m}_w (v_j - v_s) \approx 2{,}000\,\text{N at 60 knots}</math>
* '''Propulsive efficiency''': <math>\eta_p = \frac{2}{1 + v_j/v_s} \approx 72\%\text{ at } v_j/v_s = 1.5</math>

=== Micro Fusion Drive (Gen-2) ===
''Primary vehicle: [[Magneto Speeder]]''

Power chain:
<math>\text{D-T plasma} \xrightarrow{\text{fusion}} \text{14 MeV } n + \alpha \xrightarrow{\text{blanket}} \text{heat} \xrightarrow{\text{MHD}} \text{thrust}</math>

or (aneutronic mode):
<math>p + {}^{11}\text{B} \rightarrow 3\alpha \xrightarrow{\text{direct conv.}} \text{e}^- \xrightarrow{\text{MHD}} \text{thrust}</math>

* '''Power source''': [[Micro Fusion Fuel Cells]] (5–50 kW)
* '''Thrust mechanism''': Magnetohydrodynamic acceleration of ionized air
* '''MHD thrust equation''':
<math>F_{\text{MHD}} = \int_V (\mathbf{J} \times \mathbf{B})\,dV = \sigma(\mathbf{E} + \mathbf{v} \times \mathbf{B}) \times \mathbf{B} \cdot V</math>
* '''Atmospheric thrust''': ~5–15 kN (dependent on ionization level and B-field)
* '''Specific impulse''': ~3,000–8,000 s (MHD in ionized air)

=== Turbo Fusion Drive ===
Adds an exhaust-driven turbocompressor to the Micro Fusion Drive for sustained high-load cruising. The turbine extracts energy from the MHD exhaust to pre-compress intake air:

<math>P_{\text{comp}} = \dot{m}_{\text{air}} \cdot c_p \cdot T_1 \cdot \left[\left(\frac{p_2}{p_1}\right)^{(\gamma-1)/\gamma} - 1\right] / \eta_c</math>

This increases effective thrust by 30–50% during cruise by densifying the working fluid before MHD acceleration.

=== Super Fusion Drive ===
Replaces the exhaust-driven turbine with a mechanically driven supercharger powered directly from the fusion reactor's electrical output. Provides instant power surges without exhaust-temperature spool-up delay:

<math>P_{\text{boost}} = \frac{\dot{m}_{\text{air}} \cdot \Delta p}{\rho \cdot \eta_s}</math>

Adds ~50–100% thrust above baseline Micro Fusion Drive for emergency acceleration and combat maneuvers.

=== Hybrid Fusion Drive ===
Transitional variant combining Flash H₂ and Micro Fusion systems:
* '''Flash H₂''': Cold-start, low-load operations, emergency backup
* '''Micro Fusion''': Sustained high-power flight after warm-up
* '''Crossover logic''': Automatic switchover when fusion cell reaches operating temperature (~10–30 seconds)

== Thrust Vectoring ==
All Fusion Drive variants use gimbal-mounted thrust nozzles with 3-axis control:

<math>\vec{F}_{\text{net}} = \sum_{i=1}^{n} T_i \cdot \hat{n}_i(\theta_i, \phi_i)</math>

where <math>T_i</math> is individual thruster force and <math>\hat{n}_i(\theta_i, \phi_i)</math> is the unit vector determined by gimbal angles. The Magneto Speeder uses 4 independent MHD nozzles for full 6-DOF control.

== Integration with Vehicle Systems ==
* '''Power bus''': DC (Hydro: 24V/48V) or AC (Magneto: 400V 3-phase)
* '''Control interface''': [[Starcom]]/[[Navcom]] + [[Ra]] ([[PsiSys]]) for mission-aware throttling
* '''Cooling''': Seawater heat exchanger (Hydro) or liquid-metal radiative loop (Magneto)
* '''Structural''': Central mounting cradle with vibration isolation and quick-disconnect for field overhaul

== Variants Summary ==
{| class="wikitable"
|+ Fusion Drive Variant Comparison
|-
! Variant !! Power Source !! Thrust Type !! Output !! Vehicle !! Era
|-
| Flash Hydrogen || [[Flash Hydrogen Fuel Cell|Flash H₂ FC]] || Water-jet || 1.2 kW / 2 kN || [[Hydro Speeder]] || 2032+
|-
| Micro Fusion || [[Micro Fusion Fuel Cells|μ-Fusion]] || MHD || 50 kW / 15 kN || [[Magneto Speeder]] || 2035+
|-
| Turbo Fusion || μ-Fusion + turbo || MHD + compressor || 75 kW / 22 kN || Magneto (cruise) || 2038+
|-
| Super Fusion || μ-Fusion + supercharger || MHD + boost || 100+ kW / 30+ kN || Magneto (combat) || 2039+
|-
| Hybrid Fusion || Flash H₂ + μ-Fusion || Mixed || Variable || Magneto (transitional) || 2035–2040
|-
| Hyper Fusion || Aneutronic + MHD Core || Full magnetogravitic || 500+ kW || [[Star Speeder]] || 2044+
|}

title: Fusion Drives
wikitext:
[[File:Animated micro fusion turbine smooth loop.gif|thumb|right|250px|Micro Fusion turbine — continuous operation animation]]

'''Fusion Drives''' is the collective designation for the family of [[Fusion Drive]] propulsion assemblies used across [[Clan Tho'ra]] vehicles. This page provides an overview; see [[Fusion Drive]] for detailed technical specifications and equations.

== Drive Family ==
{| class="wikitable"
|+ Fusion Drive Variants
|-
! Variant !! Power Source !! Application !! Era
|-
| [[Fusion Drive#Flash Hydrogen Fusion Drive|Flash Hydrogen Fusion Drive]] || [[Flash Hydrogen Fuel Cell]]s || [[Hydro Speeder]] surface propulsion || 2032+
|-
| [[Fusion Drive#Micro Fusion Drive|Micro Fusion Drive]] || [[Micro Fusion Fuel Cells]] || [[Magneto Speeder]] atmospheric flight || 2035+
|-
| [[Fusion Drive#Turbo Fusion Drive|Turbo Fusion Drive]] || μ-Fusion + turbocompressor || Magneto Speeder sustained cruise || 2038+
|-
| [[Fusion Drive#Super Fusion Drive|Super Fusion Drive]] || μ-Fusion + supercharger || Magneto Speeder combat/sprint || 2039+
|-
| [[Fusion Drive#Hybrid Fusion Drive|Hybrid Fusion Drive]] || Flash H₂ + μ-Fusion || Transitional operations || 2035–2040
|-
| Hyper Fusion Drive || Aneutronic + [[MHD Core]] || [[Star Speeder]] interplanetary || 2044+
|}

== Technology Tree ==
Fusion Drives fall within the [[Fusion Tech]] technology tree:

* [[Fusion Cells]] — power generation modules
* [[Fusion Thrusters]] — directed thrust nozzles
* [[Fusion Turbines]] — turbocompressor enhancements
* [[Fusion Engines]] — complete engine assemblies
* [[Turbo Fusion]] — exhaust-driven boost
* [[Super Fusion]] — mechanically-driven boost
* [[Hyper Fusion]] — aneutronic / field-drive integration

== See Also ==
* [[Fusion Drive]]
* [[Flash Hydrogen Fuel Cell]]
* [[Micro Fusion Fuel Cells]]
* [[MHD Core]]
* [[Magneto Speeder]]

title: Fusion Engine Suit
wikitext:
{| class="infobox" style="width:300px; font-size:90%; border:1px solid #aaa; background:#f9f9f9; margin:0.5em 0 0.5em 1em; padding:0.2em; float:right;"
! colspan="2" style="background:#333; color:#fff;" | '''Fusion Engine Suit'''
|-
| '''Type''' || Modular psi-tech exoframe / power suit
|-
| '''Developer''' || [[Clan Tho'ra]]
|-
| '''Primary Users''' || [[Jane Tho'ra]] ([[Fusion Girl]]), [[Jono Tho'ra]] ([[Fusion Guy]]), [[Aurora Tho'ra]] ([[Fusion Gal]])
|-
| '''Power Source''' || [[Micro Fusion Fuel Cells|Micro Fusion Flash Cells]] × 6 + [[Fission Core]]
|-
| '''Flight System''' || [[Quad Wing Emission System]] → [[Fusion Wings]]
|-
| '''Interface''' || [[PsiSys]] via [[PsiLink]] mesh network
|-
| '''Era''' || [[2035]]–[[2044]]+
|-
| '''Status''' || Active — continuously iterated
|}

The '''Fusion Engine Suit''' is the signature powered exoframe worn by members of [[Clan Tho'ra]], and the reason the game is called ''[[Fusion Girl]]''. The suit is not a single fixed design — it is a modular, endlessly iterated platform with swappable components, interchangeable parts, upgrades, augmentations, and alternate versions. Wearing the suit is what makes a character "Fusion ___" — you are not [[Fusion Girl]] until you wear the Fusion Engine Suit.

== Philosophy ==
{{Main|Fusion (Philosophy)}}
The name "Fusion" extends far beyond nuclear fusion as a power source. '''Fusion''' is the [[Clan Tho'ra|Tho'ra]] way — an ''artificial'' approach to accessing [[Universal Magic]] by merging [[Psi Tech|psionic technology]] with physical engineering. Where [[Tarr'i]]'s people practice [[Natura Magic]] (the organic, holistic channeling of [[Universal Language]]), the Tho'ra ''build'' a [[Morphing Grid]] — constructing technology that interfaces with the fundamental forces of reality.

Fusion means:
* '''Nuclear Fusion''' — the literal power source at the suit's core
* '''Psionic + Technological''' — merging consciousness interfaces with hard engineering
* '''Organic + Artificial''' — biological neural networks linked to machine systems
* '''Universal Language + Physical Engineering''' — coding reality through constructed devices

== Iconic Configuration ==
The most recognized version of the Fusion Engine Suit features:

=== Power Plant ===
* '''6 [[Micro Fusion Fuel Cells|Micro Fusion Flash Cells]]''' — arranged as two rectangles of 3 cells each (left and right sides of the back)
* '''1 [[Fission Core]]''' — mounted at the lower-back center, where the two cell rectangles angle inward

The six Flash Cells operate in sequence, much like a naturally aspirated combustion engine — each cell "pops off" a flash of fusion in turn, balancing the particular matter generated by its neighbors. The [[Fission Core]] provides a counter-balancing fission reaction that stabilizes the entire fusion-fission nuclear cycle.

=== Wing System ===
* '''[[Quad Wing Emission System]]''' — positioned between the two cell rectangles
* Catches and recycles '''Hot Fusion Plasma''' into pockets of locked [[Quantum Foam]]
* The quantum foam pockets serve as '''magnetogravitic anchors''' for advanced flight and reality anchorage
* Makes the wearer a '''[[Temporal Anchor Being]]''' (artificial — compare to [[Tara Van Dekar]]'s natural temporal anchoring)

=== Wing Characteristics ===
* Default burn color: '''Amber / Ember'''
* Color, animation, form, volatility, and capability vary by upgrade, mode, state, and modification
* Operate as an '''energy buffer''' — a hyper-capacitor that the Fusion Cells cycle between
* Cells maintain a constant '''flash step''' between each other through the wing energy buffer

== Head Protection ==
The suit supports two primary head-protection paradigms:

=== Helmets ===
Fully enclosed head protection. Various models for different threat environments — vacuum-rated, blast-rated, stealth configurations.

=== HelmKits ===
{{Main|HelmKit}}
A non-enclosed headpiece that:
* '''Static-charges the wearer's hair''' for enhanced psionic sensory capability
* Can generate a '''magnetogravitic bubble''' around the head and/or body
* The magnetogravitic bubble provides stronger protection than any physical suit material
* Preferred by experienced pilots who rely on psionic awareness over physical shielding

== Variants ==
The Fusion Engine Suit exists in countless configurations. Each category below represents a different axis of variation:

=== By Version ===
{| class="wikitable"
! Designation !! User !! Era !! Notes
|-
| Proto Fusion Suit v00 || [[Jono Tho'ra]] || [[2032]]–[[2035]] || First prototype, [[Flash Hydrogen]] powered
|-
| Test Fusion Suit v01 || [[Jono Tho'ra]] || [[2035]]–[[2038]] || [[Micro Fusion]] integration
|-
| Production Suit v02 || [[Jane Tho'ra]] || [[2036]]–[[2040]] || Jane's primary suit
|-
| Super Fusion Suit v03 || [[Jono Tho'ra]] / [[Jane Tho'ra]] || [[2038]]+ || [[Super Fusion]] enhanced
|-
| Hyper Fusion Suit v04 || [[Amber Tho'ra]] || [[2044]]+ || [[Hyper Fusion]] / Star Surfer era
|}

=== By Module Type ===
{| class="wikitable sortable"
! Category !! Examples !! Description
|-
| '''Variants''' || Hot Fusion, Cold Fusion, Stealth, Aquatic, Void-rated || Fundamentally different reactor/suit architectures
|-
| '''Types''' || Combat, Recon, Engineering, Medical, Diplomatic || Mission-role configurations
|-
| '''Alternatives''' || Solid-Light Suit, Psi-Field Suit, Exoframe, Skinsuit || Different structural paradigms
|-
| '''Modules''' || Wing Module, Sensor Module, Weapon Module, Shield Module || Swappable subsystem packages
|-
| '''Components''' || Flash Cells, Fission Core, HelmKit, Repulsors || Individual replaceable parts
|-
| '''Parts''' || Plasma conduits, coolant lines, neural interfaces, seals || Maintenance-level elements
|}

=== By Operating Mode ===
{| class="wikitable"
! Mode !! Power State !! Wing Color !! Primary Use
|-
| '''Idle''' || [[Cold Fusion]] sustain || Dim amber glow || Standby, passive defense
|-
| '''Cruise''' || Micro Fusion nominal || Steady amber || Transit, patrol
|-
| '''Combat''' || Flash Fusion cycling || Bright amber/white || Active engagement
|-
| '''Turbo''' || [[Turbo Fusion]] overcharge || Pulsing orange || High-speed pursuit
|-
| '''Super''' || [[Super Fusion]] surge || Blazing white-gold || Maximum combat output
|-
| '''Hyper''' || [[Hyper Fusion]] || Dimensional shimmer || FTL / dimensional operations
|-
| '''Stealth''' || Minimal emission || Near-invisible || Infiltration
|-
| '''Anchor''' || Sustained runaway fusion || Reality-warping amber || [[Temporal Anchor Being|Temporal anchoring]], rift closure
|}

== Psionic Integration ==
The suit interfaces with [[PsiSys]] through a mesh network of [[Neural Network Hardware]] connected to [[Psi Emitter]]s. This enables:
* Communication via [[Psionic Field Projection]]
* Dynamic defense reconfiguration via [[Psi Stabilizers]]
* Sensor augmentation through the [[HelmKit]]
* [[PsiLink]] connectivity to [[PsiNet]], other Tho'ra suits, and [[Magneto Speeder]]s

The suit's internal [[AI]] operates the fusion-fission balance cycle, manages energy distribution, and can make autonomous decisions (such as generating an emergency [[Warp Zone]] if energy buildup becomes critical).

== Who Wears the Suit ==
* '''[[Jane Tho'ra]]''' — [[Fusion Girl]] (primary protagonist)
* '''[[Jono Tho'ra]]''' — [[Fusion Guy]] (mentor, original designer)
* '''[[Aurora Tho'ra]]''' — [[Fusion Gal]] (uses adapted versions)
* '''[[Amber Tho'ra]]''' — inherits advanced Hyper-era suits
* All [[Clan Tho'ra]] members share access to the suit arsenal

The Tho'ra share an '''entire arsenal''' of suits and components — any Tho'ra can wear any compatible suit configuration.

== See Also ==
* [[Fusion Tech]] — the technology family
* [[Fusion Wings]] — the wing subsystem in detail
* [[Micro Fusion Fuel Cells]] — the power cells
* [[Fission Core]] — the stabilizing reactor
* [[HelmKit]] — the psionic headpiece
* [[Quad Wing Emission System]] — the wing emission hardware
* [[Fusion Girl]] — the archetype
* [[Fusion Guy]] — Jono Tho'ra's alter ego
* [[Morphing Grid]] — the universal power framework Tho'ra tech interfaces with

title: Fusion Engines
wikitext:
'''Fusion Engines''' (also referred to as '''Fusion Powerplants''' or '''Fusion Propulsion Engines''') are the complete, integrated power and propulsion systems used in Clan Tho'ra vehicles and major facilities. They combine [[Fusion Cells]], [[Fusion Drives]], [[Fusion Thrusters]], [[Fusion Turbines]], and control electronics into a single major assembly that serves as the central "heart" of propulsion and energy delivery.

{| class="infobox" style="width:300px; font-size:90%; border:1px solid #aaa; background:#f9f9f9; margin:0.5em 0 0.5em 1em; padding:0.2em; float:right;"
! colspan="2" | '''Fusion Engines'''
|-
| '''Type''' || Fully integrated fusion power & propulsion system
|-
| '''Developer''' || Clan Tho'ra / Earth Intelligence Network (in-house R&D)
|-
| '''Manufacturer''' || In-house fabrication at Tho'ra HQ
|-
| '''Generation''' || Generation 1–4 (Flash Hydrogen to early Hyper Fusion)
|-
| '''Introduction''' || 2032–2033 (Hydro Speeder baseline)  
2035–2038 (Magneto Speeder variants)
|-
| '''Status''' || Operational (Hydro) / Prototype-to-mature (Magneto)
|-
| '''Primary User''' || Hydro Speeder fleet, Magneto Speeder fleet, Tho'ra HQ high-demand infrastructure
|-
| '''Role''' || Complete vehicle or facility power generation, propulsion, and thrust vectoring
|-
| '''Power source''' || [[Flash Hydrogen Fuel Cells]] (early) → [[Micro Fusion Fuel Cells]] (mature) + [[Turbo Fusion]] / [[Super Fusion]] enhancements
|-
| '''Propulsion output''' || Surface: 80–120 knots (Hydro)  
Atmospheric glide: Mach 0.8–1.2+ (Magneto early) → higher (mature)
|-
| '''Dimensions''' || ~2.5–4.5 m length × 1.2–2.0 m width × 1.2–2.0 m height (vehicle-dependent)
|-
| '''Weight''' || 400–1,200 kg (depending on variant and power level)
|}

== Overview ==
Fusion Engines represent the highest level of integration in Clan Tho'ra propulsion technology. They encompass the full power generation, energy conversion, thrust production, and control systems needed to operate a vehicle or major facility subsystem. Unlike individual [[Fusion Cells]] (modular power packs) or [[Fusion Drives]] (propulsion blocks), a Fusion Engine is the complete, self-contained "powerplant" that delivers both energy and directed motion.

Early Fusion Engines (2032–2035) are based on [[Flash Hydrogen Fuel Cells]] for surface-aquatic mobility. Later generations (2035–2040+) incorporate [[Micro Fusion Fuel Cells]] with [[Turbo Fusion]] or [[Super Fusion]] enhancements for atmospheric and high-demand operations. Theoretical [[Hyper Fusion]] variants remain conceptual until the Star Surfer era.

== Design & Specifications ==
* '''Core power''' — Flash Hydrogen Fuel Cells (early) or Micro Fusion Fuel Cells (mature)
* '''Propulsion''' — Water-jet (Hydro) or magneto-hydrodynamic / magneto-gravitic (Magneto)
* '''Thrust vectoring''': Array of [[Fusion Thrusters]] with gimbal or electromagnetic control
* '''Performance (Hydro)''':
** Surface speed: 80–120 knots
** Range: 150–250 nautical miles
* '''Performance (Magneto)''':
** Glide speed: Mach 0.8–1.2+ (early variants)
** Altitude capability: 0–10,000 m (early); higher with maturation
* '''Dimensions''': Vehicle-dependent (typically 2.5–4.5 m long × 1.2–2.0 m wide × 1.2–2.0 m high)
* '''Weight''': 400–1,200 kg (increases with power level and enhancements)
* '''Cooling''': Liquid-metal loops + radiative panels
* '''Control interface''': Integrated with Starcom/Navcom and Ra (PsiSys) for mission-aware operation

== Variants ==
* '''Flash Hydrogen Fusion Engine''' — Baseline surface-aquatic engine using Flash Hydrogen Fuel Cells.
* '''Micro Fusion Engine''' — High-energy atmospheric engine using Micro Fusion Fuel Cells.
* '''Turbo Fusion Engine''' — Enhanced with exhaust-driven turbines for sustained high-load performance.
* '''Super Fusion Engine''' — Enhanced with mechanically driven supercharger for instant power surges.
* '''Hybrid Fusion Engine''' — Combines Flash Hydrogen + Micro Fusion for transitional performance.
* '''Hyper Fusion Engine''' — Theoretical future variant incorporating dimensional field generation (post-2044).

== Operational Use ==
* '''Hydro Speeder''' — Complete powerplant for surface water traversal, coastal reconnaissance, and rapid deployment.
* '''Magneto Speeder''' — Primary propulsion system for low-altitude gliding, zone insertion/extraction, and high-speed evasion.
* '''Tho'ra HQ high-demand infrastructure''': Powers large fabrication bays, symbology arrays, psi-tech chambers, and defensive systems.
* '''Forward outpost power''': Deployed in mobile or temporary bases for sustained high-output needs.

== Development History ==
* '''2032–2033''': First Flash Hydrogen Fusion Engines integrated into Hydro Speeder prototypes at Tho'ra HQ.
* '''2033–2035''': Operational maturity for water-edge missions; fleet expanded.
* '''2035–2038''': First Micro Fusion Engine prototypes tested in Magneto Speeder platforms.
* '''2038–2040''': Turbo and Super variants added for performance enhancement.
* '''2040+''': Hybrid and early Hyper Fusion concepts begin testing for cosmic-scale missions.

== Advantages & Limitations ==
* '''Advantages''':
** Complete, self-contained power + propulsion solution
** Scalable from surface to atmospheric and (future) cosmic operations
** Integrates all component types ([[Cells]], [[Drives]], [[Thrusters]], [[Turbines]])
* '''Limitations''':
** Large and heavy compared to individual components
** Requires significant downtime for major repairs or upgrades
** High complexity in hybrid or advanced variants

= FusionGirl =

''FusionGirl'' is more than just a person—it is a universal archetype representing heroism, adaptability, and the mastery of cosmic forces. Across countless timelines and multiverses, the identity of FusionGirl manifests in individuals who rise to meet the challenges of their worlds. The archetype is rooted in the mastery of [[Universal Language]] and [[PsiTech]], making it both a role to fill and an ideal to aspire to.

[[Jane Tho'ra]] is [[The Fusion Girl]] - Trained by her mentor [[Jono Tho'ra]] the [[Fusion Guy]]

== Overview ==
FusionGirl is an archetype, akin to mythical figures like Batman or Wonder Woman. She embodies the balance between individuality and collective responsibility, acting as a beacon of hope and transformation in a fractured multiverse. 

While [[Jane Tho'ra]] is the most iconic manifestation of FusionGirl in the current timeline, others have stepped into the role in different realities.

=== FusionGirl in Mythology ===
The archetype of FusionGirl draws parallels to mythological figures who transcend their individual identities to become symbols of transformation, such as Athena or Saraswati.

=== Connection to Psi and Tech ===
FusionGirl is unique in her ability to merge psychic energy (Psi) with advanced technology, symbolizing a harmony between intuition and innovation.

== The Archetype ==
=== Definition ===
FusionGirl represents the concept of a multiversal hero who embraces change, overcomes adversity, and redefines reality through the application of both courage and advanced knowledge.

=== Symbolism ===
* **Heroism by Choice:** Unlike many archetypes, FusionGirl’s journey is not defined by trauma but by the deliberate decision to step into a larger purpose.
* **Balance of Forces:** FusionGirl wields [[Universal Language]] and [[PsiTech]] to harmonize chaos and create order.
* **Multiversal Resonance:** Across timelines, FusionGirl represents unity, adaptability, and the potential for greatness within all beings.

=== Archetype vs. Identity ===
FusionGirl is both an archetype and a role. While individuals like [[Jane Tho'ra]] fill the role, the archetype itself transcends any single character.

=== Evolution of the Archetype ===
The concept of FusionGirl evolves across timelines. In some realities, she is a savior; in others, a tragic figure who loses her way.

== Key Characteristics ==
=== Heroism Through Choice ===
FusionGirl’s identity is shaped by her decision to act, rather than being driven by external circumstances. This makes her relatable and accessible to anyone seeking to find meaning in their actions.

=== The Universal Language ===
The mastery of Universal Language is a defining trait of FusionGirl. It allows her to:
* Decode the fabric of the multiverse.
* Solve intricate cosmic puzzles.
* Forge connections between diverse beings and dimensions.

=== PsiTech ===
FusionGirl combines psychic energy and advanced technology, using PsiTech to:
* Navigate timelines and dimensions.
* Harness emotional intelligence in battle and diplomacy.
* Adapt dynamically to multiversal threats.

=== [[Magneto Speeder]] ===

[[File:MagnetoSpeeder FusionGirl StoppingRightOverWater-01a.png|816px|thumb|none|FusionGirl - MagnetoSpeeder-Cruiser Type-RX]]

[[File:MagnetoSpeeder-SmallType-SX1-01a.png|thumb|none|MagnetoSpeeder-Small Type-SX1]]

Jane Tho'ra rides a [[Magneto Speeder]] that eventually evolves into a [[Star Speeder]].
[[Magneto Speeders]] each have a [[Persona Core]] that operates as an [[AI Security RelayNode]] to prevent theft and manage auto navigation.

* [[Fusion Girl - The Game: Pilot Mode]] is about flying a [[Magneto Speeder]] along side [[Battle Dragon]]s and [[Psisys]] [[Dino Bot]]s

=== [[Fusion Wings]] ===
[[File:FusionGirl-MirageStealthField-FusionWings-TypeD-256x.gif|thumb|none|FusionGirl-MirageStealthField-FusionWings-TypeD]]

=== [[Plasma Morphers]] ===

=== Emotional Depth ===
FusionGirl’s emotional intelligence, often enhanced through PsiTech, allows her to form deep connections with allies and adversaries alike.

=== Adaptability ===
As an archetype, FusionGirl thrives in any reality, adapting to its unique challenges while maintaining her core values.

== The Multiverse ==
FusionGirl’s presence is felt across countless realities, though her form, abilities, and role may differ:
* In some timelines, FusionGirl is a hero, leading worlds to salvation.
* In darker realities, the archetype is corrupted, becoming a symbol of chaos.
* The "Prime FusionGirl" timeline, featuring [[Jane Tho'ra]], represents the ultimate realization of the archetype.

=== FusionGirl Across Realities ===
Each reality offers a unique manifestation of FusionGirl, influenced by the environment, culture, and timeline’s history.

=== The Role of Universal Forces ===
Cosmic entities and forces often play a part in the emergence of FusionGirl, guiding her development across timelines.

== Manifestations ==
FusionGirl is not a single person but a recurring identity filled by those who align with its ideals. While [[Jane Tho'ra]] is the most prominent example, the archetype has appeared in various forms:
* **The Proto FusionGirl:** Early manifestations of the archetype, exploring its potential.
* **The Prime FusionGirl:** The ultimate form of FusionGirl, fully realizing her connection to Universal Language and PsiTech.

=== Archetype in Crisis ===
In some realities, the archetype of FusionGirl fails, leading to fractured timelines or darker outcomes.

== [[PsiSys]] and [[Tho'ra]] ==
Together with the entire [[Tho'ra Continuum]], Jane works to become the Fusion Girl and save the future from trans-dimensional and super cosmic peril.

[[Tho'ra]] all have a special bond and connection with the lifeforms across the cosmos, including [[Mecha]] and [[Digital]] life. The new [[AI Factions]] that manifested from after the [[Time Wars]] made alliances with various factions, often including the [[Tho'ra Clan]] and [[Tho'ra Continuum]].

The [[Beu]], [[Psisys]] and [[Tho'ra]] work together as friends on a mission to save the future.

=== Archetype as Catalyst ===
Even when FusionGirl is not the main force for change, her presence often catalyzes transformative events in the multiverse.

== Legacy ==
FusionGirl’s legacy extends beyond individuals. She represents the possibility of unity and transformation, inspiring others to seek their own paths toward greatness. Her actions ripple across timelines, affecting the very fabric of the multiverse.

=== The Impact of Choice ===
FusionGirl’s legacy underscores the power of choice. Her journey proves that small decisions can create profound multiversal ripples.

=== An Eternal Archetype ===
FusionGirl remains an eternal figure, a beacon for those who seek to rise above their circumstances and challenge the status quo.

== Trivia ==
* The name "FusionGirl" reflects the blending of psychic, technological, and cosmic elements.
* The archetype was first recognized by [[Jono Tho'ra]], who identified its potential in [[Jane Tho'ra]].
* FusionGirl’s mastery of [[Universal Language]] makes her one of the most versatile and powerful figures in the multiverse.

== See Also ==

= FusionGirl Game =

''FusionGirl Game'' is a sci-fi action-adventure video game that immerses players in the multiverse through the eyes of [[Jane Tho'ra]]. The game explores themes of choice, heroism, and the mysteries of [[Universal Language]], offering dynamic, replayable gameplay and an emotionally gripping narrative.

== Overview ==
''FusionGirl Game'' challenges players to navigate procedurally generated timelines, solve intricate puzzles, and master [[PsiTech]] to reshape the multiverse. The game blends innovative mechanics with deep storytelling, allowing players to define their journey through every choice they make.

=== Genre ===
The game combines elements of:
* Sci-Fi Action-Adventure
* Narrative-Driven Role-Playing
* Puzzle and Exploration

=== Core Themes ===
* **Heroism Through Choice:** Players explore the idea that heroism comes not from trauma but from deliberate, personal decisions.
* **The Multiverse:** Dive into infinite realities, each shaped by unique challenges, allies, and threats.
* **Mastery of Universal Language:** Unlock the secrets of the multiverse through puzzles and communication with cosmic beings.

== Gameplay Mechanics ==
''FusionGirl Game'' incorporates cutting-edge systems to create an engaging player experience.

=== Timestream Framework ===
The Timestream Framework dynamically generates timelines and missions, ensuring no two playthroughs are the same. Players can:
* Alter outcomes through key decisions.
* Explore parallel realities based on their choices.
* Unlock hidden timelines by solving advanced puzzles.

=== PsiTech ===
Players harness [[PsiTech]] to:
* Manipulate environments.
* Engage in psychic combat.
* Synchronize with allies and enemies to unlock new abilities.

=== Emotional AI ===
The game features advanced AI systems that:
* Adapt NPC interactions to the player's choices and emotional state.
* Create deeper connections with allies and adversaries.
* Drive dynamic story progression.

== Story and Setting ==
=== Narrative ===
The story follows [[Jane Tho'ra]] as she embarks on a journey to uncover her potential as [[FusionGirl]], navigate the multiverse, and confront cosmic threats like the [[Draken Seforthi]]. Her mentor, [[Jono Tho'ra]], guides her in mastering PsiTech and Universal Language.

=== Multiversal Exploration ===
Players traverse a richly detailed multiverse, featuring:
* Ley Line Networks connecting key nodes of power.
* Fractured timelines influenced by player actions.
* Hidden dimensions requiring advanced problem-solving to access.

=== Key Adversaries ===
* **Draken Seforthi:** A malevolent species threatening to destabilize the multiverse.
* **Malefic PsiSys Robots:** Rogue AI creations that burrow into planetary cores, creating existential crises.

== Development and Vision ==
=== Creator's Vision ===
''FusionGirl Game'' was envisioned by [[Jordan Traña]] to bring the concept of Universal Language and multiversal exploration to life. The project serves as both a narrative and an experimental framework for showcasing the possibilities of advanced game mechanics.

=== Meta-Narrative ===
In certain realities within the game, [[Jordan Traña]] is revealed to have created the ''FusionGirl Game'' and simultaneously become [[Jono Tho'ra]]. These "meta-realities" represent the best possible timelines, achievable only through the hardest difficulties and most complex missions.

=== Technology ===
''FusionGirl Game'' leverages:
* **Procedural Generation:** Ensures replayability and endless possibilities.
* **Blockchain Integration:** Powers a decentralized modding ecosystem where players can create and share content.
* **AI Frameworks:** Enhance NPC behavior, story branching, and emotional depth.

== Features ==
=== Difficulty Levels ===
The game offers scalable difficulty levels, with higher challenges unlocking unique content, including:
* Alternate realities.
* Insights into the meta-narrative.
* "Prime FusionGirl" endings.

=== Modding Tools ===
A decentralized modding system allows players to:
* Create custom missions and timelines.
* Share mods securely through blockchain technology.
* Expand the game’s universe collaboratively.

=== Rewards and Unlockables ===
Players earn unique rewards, including:
* New PsiTech abilities.
* Rare timeline nodes.
* Special lore entries that expand the multiverse's backstory.

== Legacy ==
''FusionGirl Game'' aims to inspire players to explore their own potential and the interconnectedness of reality. It challenges traditional notions of gaming by integrating cutting-edge technologies and a meta-narrative that bridges fiction and reality.

=== Cultural Impact ===
The game explores the idea of Universal Language as a tool for understanding, connection, and empowerment, positioning it as both a narrative and philosophical cornerstone.

=== Best Possible Timelines ===
Players who reach the highest difficulty levels unlock the "best possible timelines," where the multiverse achieves harmony and [[Jane Tho'ra]] fully realizes her potential as the Prime FusionGirl.

== Trivia ==
* The game includes hidden references to [[Jordan Traña]]’s real-world inspiration for the narrative.
* Certain missions include easter eggs from earlier timelines, such as Jono Tho'ra’s adventures.
* [[Jane Tho'ra]]’s synchronized Psi Music scene is considered one of the most iconic moments in the game.

== See Also ==
* [[Jane Tho'ra]]
* [[FusionGirl]]
* [[Jono Tho'ra]]
* [[Universal Language]]
* [[Draken Seforthi]]

== External Links ==
* [FusionGirl Official Website](https://fusiongirl.app)
* [FusionGirl Wiki](https://wiki.fusiongirl.app)

The '''Fusion Team''' is the core operative squad of [[Clan Tho'ra]] — the team that carries the "Fusion" designation and fights together using [[Fusion Tech]].

== Members ==
{| class="wikitable"
! Designation !! Character !! Role
|-
| [[Fusion Girl]] || [[Jane Tho'ra]] || Team leader, primary protagonist
|-
| [[Fusion Guy]] || ''TBD'' || Team member
|-
| [[Fusion Gal]] || ''TBD'' || Team member
|-
| [[Fusion Boy]] || ''TBD'' || Team member
|}

== Equipment ==
All Fusion Team members share access to the [[Clan Tho'ra|Tho'ra]] arsenal:
* '''[[Fusion Engine Suit]]''' — the core powered exosuit
* '''[[Fusion Wings]]''' — all wing types, constantly iterated
* '''[[Star Morphers]]''' — Power Ranger-style transformation devices provisioned by the [[Morphing Grid]]
* '''[[Magneto Speeder]]s''' — personal vehicles summoned via Star Morphers
* '''[[Fusion Weapons]]''' — [[Fusion Blades]], [[Fusion Cutters]], [[Psi Blades]], [[Super Blades]]
* '''[[HelmKit]]''' — psionic helmet system with [[PsiLink]] mesh integration

== Team Dynamics ==
The Fusion Team operates as a coordinated unit, with each member bringing different strengths. All members share the same wing arsenal — the Tho'ra constantly build new wing iterations that the entire team benefits from.
